title: More Comments on Linear Systems
summary: This page demonstrates that systems described by general N-th order linear differential equations (with constant or time-varying coefficients) are linear systems exhibiting zero-state linearity, zero-input linearity, and the decomposition property. It includes two drill problems asking students to verify linearity and nonlinearity of specific differential equations. The page also discusses why linear system analysis is powerful, noting that superposition and decomposition properties simplify analysis even though most real systems become nonlinear under large signals.
